title: Punishment for attempting to commit offences punishable with imprisonment for life or imprisonment
content: Whoever attempts to commit an offence punishable by this Code with imprisonment for life or imprisonment, or to cause such an offence to be committed, and in such attempt does any act towards the commission of the offence, shall... be punished with imprisonment of any description provided for the offence, for a term which may extend to one-half of the longest term provided for that offence...

footnotes.text: The Proximity Test: This omnibus provision catches all inchoate crimes not explicitly provided for elsewhere (like Section 307 for attempted murder). To move from mere 'preparation' to a criminal 'attempt' under Section 511, the actor must cross the line by executing an overt step directly pointing to the completion of the crime.
